allowed_mutable_fields.rule: may be regenerated from canonical state bytes
may be null, empty-equivalent, or point to declared prior trace/readout refs

allowed_mutable_fields.reason: phase is selector input; unrecognized phase must be declared as expected pressure, not hidden behavior
allowed_mutable_fields.rule: may only be one of registry phases or the explicit underdeclared edge token NO_APPLICABLE_MOVE_PROBE
may remain READY or be TERMINAL_MARKER_ONLY for explicit prior-terminal marker case

forbidden_mutations.reason: changing active object would become fixture/object expansion
changing registry would become move addition or registry swap
changing schema archive would become schema mutation or authority swap
changing admissibility surface would become authority widening/substitution
changing sidecar ref belongs to later observability-status contract, not state variant rules
state variant rules may not alter runtime behavior
would create schema expansion before receipt pressure

must_not_infer: new active objects may be introduced
selection_rule: variants must be human-declared, bounded, derived from clean smoke fixed refs, and listed in the allowed catalog before execution
